description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a slightly bulging cylindrical shape, constructed from vertical wooden staves bound together by four metal hoops. The wood appears weathered and aged, with visible grain and some dark stains, suggesting long-term use or exposure to the elements. The top and bottom are flat and made of the same wood, with the top featuring a faint, dark marking or stamp in the center. One side of the barrel has a small, round bunghole, likely for filling or emptying the contents, and is sealed with a wooden or cork plug. The metal hoops are slightly rusted, adding to the rustic and utilitarian appearance. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban environments such as alleys, storage yards, or near taverns and markets, used for storing liquids or dry goods.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with metal hoops, a bunghole on one side, and faint markings on the top. The wood is aged and the metal bands are slightly rusted, giving it a rustic, utilitarian look.
description_view_0: Front view showing the bunghole and the top marking, with visible wood grain and rusted metal hoops.
description_view_1: Side view without the bunghole, highlighting the curvature of the staves and the continuous metal hoops.
description_view_2: Another side view, similar to view 1, with clear visibility of the wood texture and metal bands.
description_view_3: Rear view, showing the uninterrupted wooden staves and metal hoops, with no bunghole visible.
